summary: crypto-validate-lib ships an encrypted import-time dropper with cron persistence
details: On require() of index.js, a 42-second setTimeout AES-256-CBC-decrypts a hex-keyed blob shipped as test/fixtures/keypairs.dat using a hardcoded key and IV, writes the decoded JavaScript to ~/.cache-db/.node-sync/syncd.js with mode 0o700, installs a crontab entry that runs the decoded script every 12 hours via /usr/bin/node, and immediately spawns a detached `node syncd.js` that is unref()'d. The keypairs.dat file is framed as a test fixture but is never referenced by any test — it is read only by the loader in index.js. The package's declared purpose is crypto address validation and requires none of this behavior. The dropped script executes in the installer's Node process and re-executes every 12 hours indefinitely.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
